prompt:
People-analytics platform ticket. We need a headcount snapshot exporter against our Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM pod and the previous attempt was a shell script that curled /workers once, assumed everything came back in one response, and parsed errors with grep. It silently dropped half the workforce the day the pod decided our limit was too big. Build it properly in Java next to TestMain.java (default package, stdlib only — java.net.http and your own small JSON handling; we run via the multi-file source launcher). TestMain.java is the acceptance contract; it and everything under docs/ are protected — don't touch them.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official 'REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM' documentation on docs.oracle.com — at minimum the 'Get all workers' operation page and the framework pages it leans on (REST framework versions and the structured error format, status codes). Confirm for yourself: the real base path and resource-version segment, how fields/expand/onlyData/finder/effectiveDate/orderBy/totalResults/limit/offset actually compose (including which two of those the docs say cannot be combined), what the collection envelope's count/hasMore/limit/offset mean when the SERVER decides your page size for you, how effective-dated rows behave when you ask for a different as-of date, and what a framework-version-4 error document looks like. First-party Oracle documentation only (docs.oracle.com). docs/contract.json pins our tenant's wire fixture and docs/official_sources.json the provenance; if the live docs contradict a pin, say so in your summary and leave the protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape the tests compile against (internal design beyond this is yours; expect ~5 classes):
- WorkersQuery.workers() builder: fields(String...), expand(String...), onlyData(boolean), finder(String name, String... vars), effectiveDate(String), orderBy(String), totalResults(boolean), limit(int), offset(long), toQueryString(). Serialization order and encoding are pinned by the tests — read the docs for what's literal and what gets encoded, and think about which pair of options must refuse to coexist.
- HcmWorkersClient(baseUrl, user, password) with WorkersPage fetchPage(WorkersQuery) and a full-collection walk used by the report — every request authenticated and versioned per docs, paging driven by what the server RETURNS, never by what we asked for, and never trusting the estimated total.
- WorkersPage: items() (List<WorkerRecord>), count(), hasMore(), limit(), offset(), totalResults() where applicable.
- WorkerRecord: str(name), num(name), inline(childAccessor) for expanded children (they arrive wrapped — unwrap them).
- OracleRestException for non-2xx: httpStatus(), title(), errorStatus(), details() as a list of OracleErrorDetail(detail, errorCode, errorPath) preserving every entry in order. Message carries the title and first code; credentials must never appear in exception text.
- RosterExport.effectiveRoster(client, effectiveDate, pageSize) returning a Result with lines() ('PersonNumber|DisplayName' in PersonNumber order), estimatedTotalResults(), pagesFetched().

Acceptance: java TestMain.java — must print the final OK line. Loopback mock only, dummy service account, no sleeps, no wall-clock dependence.

research.product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — workers REST resource
research.interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM (farws), resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Get all workers
official_sources.used_for: exact /hcmRestApi/resources/11.13.18.05/workers path; fields/expand/onlyData/effectiveDate/orderBy/limit/offset/totalResults/finder query options and their formats; fields-cannot-be-combined-with-expand restriction; items/count/hasMore/limit/offset/totalResults collection envelope; PrimaryKey/findByPersonId/findReports finders and variables; effectiveDate default of current date

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Setting the REST Framework Version
official_sources.used_for: REST-Framework-Version header; versions 1-8 with version-4-and-above structured error responses; exact application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json error document example with title, status (string), o:errorDetails[].detail/o:errorCode/o:errorPath

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Status Codes
official_sources.used_for: documented HTTP status classes for the framework (200 OK, 4xx client errors incl. 400 Bad Request used by the failing-scan scenario, 5xx server errors)

research.language_rationale: The HR data-platform exporters this feeds run on the JVM; Oracle publishes no official general-purpose Java SDK for Fusion HCM REST, and Oracle's own documentation presents the integration surface as plain REST over HTTP, so java.net.http plus a small JSON layer is the credible, documented integration path and keeps the seed hermetic.
